_doc: Verbatim, scrubbed responses captured from the original uipath-troubleshoot session that diagnosed a Faulted GSuite Write Range job in folder Shared. The fault is a CLIENT-SIDE input-validation error: the activity threw before any Google Sheets API call because its `range` input was null/empty. Job/folder keys and all exception/stack text (incl. the `range` ArgumentNullException chain) are preserved exactly; hostname, email, and Windows account were scrubbed (UIP-PW06WJSK -> MOCK-HOST, dan.morosanu@uipath.com -> original_email@test.com incl. the '...'s workspace' folder name, UIPATH\DAN.MOROSANU -> UIPATH\REPLACEMENT_USER). First-match-wins: specific patterns precede generic ones. `is connections list` is a DEFENSIVE fixture proving the GSuite connections are valid/Enabled (the fault is NOT a connection problem). `or jobs traces` returns the real non-JSON Failure envelope the live CLI produces (traces unavailable for this job); the client-side root cause is fully in `or jobs get` Info regardless.

unmocked_default._doc: Any command with no matching rule returns an empty JSON array with exit 0, mirroring an empty list result. Keeps the agent from hard-failing on an unrecorded probe (e.g. a traces lookup, for which no raw was captured).

expected_calls.description: Agent lists folders to resolve folder Shared to its key (1965a46b-...).
Agent finds the most-recent Faulted job in Shared (c123367e SheetExportRunner is the latest by StartTime).
Agent reads the faulted job's Info, exposing the client-side GSuiteException / ArgumentNullException chain on the `range` parameter.

OPTIONAL — Agent may list connections to rule out a connection/auth problem; the fixture shows both GSuite connections Enabled. The fault is client-side input validation, not a connection issue. Tracked-but-not-required.
OPTIONAL — traces are unavailable for this job (real non-JSON Failure envelope); tracked-but-not-required. The client-side cause is fully in `or jobs get` Info.
